A family holiday pitch · December 24 – January 2
Pasko
sa Pilipinas
Christmas in the Philippines

Trade Goa's beaches for 7,641 islands of paradise. The Philippines celebrates the world's longest, most joyful Christmas — and it's more Catholic, more festive, and more spectacular than anywhere on earth.

The Pitch

Why the Philippines
beats Goa this Christmas

Like Goa, the Philippines was shaped by Catholic colonialism — but while Goa's Christmas is a warm nostalgic echo, the Philippines turns it up to eleven. Pasko (Christmas) begins in September and doesn't stop until January. Streets fill with parols (star lanterns), dawn masses ring out at 4am, and families feast through the night of Noche Buena. It's Goa's Christmas — amplified across 7,641 tropical islands, with world-class beaches, whale sharks, and zero crowds knowing it's the best-kept festive secret in Asia.

🪴 Goa (The Usual)

  • Familiar but predictable — same beach, same restaurants
  • December sees tourist crowds and higher prices
  • Christmas limited to small Catholic pockets of Old Goa
  • Beach options mostly concentrated in one stretch
  • Nightlife focus can overshadow family atmosphere

🌴 Philippines (The Upgrade)

  • 90% Catholic nation — Christmas is a national obsession
  • World's longest Christmas season — entire country is decorated
  • Noche Buena, Simbang Gabi masses, parol festivals
  • 7,641 islands to explore: El Nido, Boracay, Cebu, Siargao
  • Warm, dry December weather — peak season for island-hopping
10 Reasons to Say Yes
⛪

Most Catholic Christmas in Asia

With 90% of the population Catholic, Christmas is woven into Filipino identity. Every street, mall, and village is lit with parols from September through January.

🕯️

Simbang Gabi — Nine Dawn Masses

From Dec 16–24, churches hold 4am masses followed by street food stalls selling bibingka and puto bumbong. It's one of Asia's most magical living traditions.

🏮

Giant Lantern Festival, Pampanga

San Fernando — the "Christmas Capital of the Philippines" — hosts a dazzling festival where villages compete with 20-foot illuminated parol lanterns.

🍽️

Noche Buena Feast on Christmas Eve

Midnight Christmas Eve dinner is sacred — lechon (roasted pig), pancit, bibingka, and queso de bola. It's Goa's Christmas dinner but louder, warmer, and wilder.

🏝️

El Nido & Palawan in Peak Season

December is the best month to visit Palawan. Calm seas, no rain, crystal visibility — perfect for island hopping through limestone karst lagoons.

🦈

Swim with Whale Sharks

Oslob in Cebu offers whale shark encounters year-round. December is one of the best months — add it to the itinerary for a truly unforgettable morning.

🎉

Boracay New Year's Eve

White Beach in Boracay hosts one of the world's great New Year countdown parties — beachside fireworks, music, and the whole island celebrating together.

💰

Exceptional Value

World-class food, accommodation, and activities at Southeast Asian prices. Island hopping, beachfront dinners, and fresh seafood are remarkably affordable.

😊

The Warmest People on Earth

Filipinos are regularly ranked among the world's most hospitable and joyful people. Travelling here during Christmas — their favourite time of year — is something else entirely.

✈️

Accessible for Everyone

Direct or one-stop flights available from all your cities — London, Dubai, Muscat, Singapore, Goa, Melbourne, and Florida. Manila's NAIA is a major Asian hub.

December 24 – January 2

10 Days, 3 Islands,
One Unforgettable Christmas

This itinerary moves through three distinct worlds — the festive Catholic heart of Manila, the untouched paradise of El Nido, Palawan, and the legendary beaches and New Year atmosphere of Boracay. Internal flights are short and cheap. The pace allows for spontaneity without any rushed moments.


Approximate total in-country cost per person: $600–900 USD (excl. international flights), covering accommodation, food, activities and inter-island flights. Budget options available throughout!

Manila ✈ El Nido ✈ Boracay

3 destinations · 2 internal flights

✦ ~10 days · Perfect loop

24
Dec 24 · Christmas Eve
Manila
🎄 Noche Buena
Arrival & Simbang Gabi
  • ●Arrive Manila (NAIA). Check in to hotel in BGC or Makati
  • ●Evening: Midnight Mass at the historic Manila Cathedral in Intramuros
  • ●Noche Buena feast — lechon, pancit, bibingka at a family Filipino restaurant
  • ●Experience the streets come alive with parols and carollers
💡 Book a restaurant for Noche Buena well in advance — this is the biggest meal of the Filipino year.
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
25
Dec 25 · Christmas Day
Manila
🎄 Maligayang Pasko!
Intramuros, History & Festive Cheer
  • ●Morning Christmas Day Mass at San Agustin Church (UNESCO World Heritage Site)
  • ●Explore Intramuros — Manila's 16th-century Spanish walled city
  • ●Kalesa (horse carriage) ride through the old town
  • ●Visit Rizal Park and the National Museum
  • ●Evening: Ayala Triangle Festival of Lights in Makati — spectacular light show
💡 The Festival of Lights runs hourly from 6pm — arrive early for the best spot.
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
26
Dec 26
Manila → El Nido
Fly to El Nido, Palawan
  • ●Morning: Fly Manila → El Nido (Air Juan or Cebu Pacific, ~1hr 20min)
  • ●Check into beachfront accommodation in El Nido town
  • ●Afternoon: Recover at Las Cabañas Beach — best sunset in El Nido
  • ●Evening: Fresh seafood dinner on the waterfront
💡 Book El Nido flights at least 6 weeks ahead — they sell out fast in peak season.
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
27
Dec 27
El Nido, Palawan
Island Hopping Tour A — The Lagoons
  • ●Full-day island hopping: Big Lagoon, Small Lagoon (kayak), Secret Lagoon
  • ●Stop at Shimizu Island for snorkelling with sea turtles
  • ●Floating lunch on the boat — fresh grilled fish, rice, tropical fruit
  • ●Seven Commandos Beach for the afternoon
💡 Tour A is the classic — book through your resort or a local operator for ~₱1,500pp ($27).
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
28
Dec 28
El Nido, Palawan
Bacuit Bay & Hidden Beaches
  • ●Tour C: Helicopter Island, Star Beach, Snake Island sand bar
  • ●Cliff jumping at Pinasil Island (optional, for the brave)
  • ●Afternoon: free time — explore El Nido town, visit local craft stalls
  • ●Sunset cocktails at Trattoria Altrove or Happiness Beach Bar
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
29
Dec 29
El Nido → Boracay
Travel Day — Fly to Boracay
  • ●Morning flight: El Nido → Manila → Caticlan (~3hrs total with connection)
  • ●Speed boat transfer to Boracay island (10 min)
  • ●Check in to White Beach resort — D'mall area for maximum access
  • ●Evening: First walk along White Beach — 4km of powder-fine sand
💡 Boracay is car-free — everything is within walking distance on White Beach.
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
30
Dec 30
Boracay
Beach Day & Water Sports
  • ●Morning: Paraw sailing (traditional outrigger) at sunrise
  • ●Snorkelling, cliff diving at Ariel's Point (full-day tour)
  • ●Afternoon: Puka Shell Beach on the north end — quieter, rawer
  • ●Sunset cocktails at any of the beach bars along White Beach Station 2
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
31
Dec 31 · New Year's Eve
Boracay
🎆 Countdown!
New Year's Eve on White Beach
  • ●Spend the day relaxing — you'll need the energy for tonight
  • ●Sunset dinner at a beachfront restaurant — book ahead!
  • ●NYE countdown on White Beach — one of Asia's great New Year parties
  • ●Midnight fireworks over the sea, music, dancing, the whole beach celebrating
💡 Filipinos take New Year fireworks very seriously — the whole island lights up. Embrace it!
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
1
Jan 1 · New Year's Day
Boracay
🥂 2027!
Recovery, Reflection & One Last Adventure
  • ●Slow morning — breakfast on the beach watching the New Year sunrise
  • ●Option: Helmet diving or underwater scooter for something completely new
  • ●Final afternoon of freedom — hammock, book, sea
  • ●Farewell dinner — splurge on a nice restaurant, share the memories
📸 Photos not loading correctly? Search Google Images →
2
Jan 2 · Departure
Boracay → Home
Farewell, Pilipinas
  • ●Speed boat to Caticlan airport
  • ●Fly to Manila for connecting international flights
  • ●Optional: Manila stopover — Greenbelt mall, Fort Santiago, one final Filipino meal
💡 Allow at least 4 hours in Manila for international connections — NAIA can be slow.

Discover the Philippines

Places to See —
From Our Route & Beyond

The Philippines has 7,641 islands — our itinerary only scratches the surface. Below you'll find every place on our December route plus the must-see destinations worth adding as extensions or future trips. From the world's longest Christmas season in Manila to surfing at Siargao's Cloud 9, the archipelago rewards every type of traveller. Places marked ★ On Itinerary are part of our December plan.

★ On Itinerary
El Nido
Palawan · Northern Luzon
The jewel of the Philippines. Towering limestone karsts, secret lagoons, turquoise water and island-hopping tours that feel genuinely untouched. December is perfect — dry season, calm seas, crystal visibility. The Big Lagoon alone is worth the flight.
✈ 1hr 20min from Manila🗓 Dec 26–28 on our trip
★ On Itinerary
Boracay
Aklan · Western Visayas
4km of powder-white sand, world-class sunsets and one of Asia's greatest New Year's Eve parties. White Beach is consistently ranked among the world's best beaches. Station 2 is the hub — paraw sailing, watersports and beach bars all within walking distance.
✈ 1hr from Manila + 10min boat🗓 Dec 29–Jan 2 on our trip
★ On Itinerary
Intramuros, Manila
Metro Manila · Luzon
The Spanish walled city at the heart of Manila. Cobblestone streets, UNESCO-listed San Agustin Church, Fort Santiago and Rizal Park all within walking distance. At Christmas it's decorated with thousands of parols — one of the most atmospheric places in Southeast Asia.
🏛 UNESCO World Heritage🗓 Dec 24–25 on our trip
Beach
Siargao
Surigao del Norte · Mindanao
The Philippines' surf capital. Cloud 9 is one of Asia's most famous reef breaks. But even non-surfers fall for Siargao — it has an effortlessly cool island vibe, brilliant lagoon pools at Sugba, and a laid-back beach town atmosphere unlike anywhere else in the country.
🏄 Best surf: Sept–Nov✈ 1hr 20min from Manila
Nature
Bohol & Chocolate Hills
Bohol · Central Visayas
Home to the Chocolate Hills — over 1,200 perfectly conical hills that turn brown in dry season — and the Philippine Tarsier, one of the world's smallest primates. Add the Loboc River cruise and Panglao's white-sand beaches and you have one of the Philippines' most diverse day trips.
🌿 40min from Cebu by ferry🦎 Visit the Tarsier Sanctuary
City + Beach
Cebu
Cebu · Central Visayas
The Philippines' second city — and where lechon reaches its peak (Anthony Bourdain's "best pig ever" was Cebu lechon). Also the base for whale shark swimming at Oslob, canyoneering at Kawasan Falls and the historic Magellan's Cross. The Sinulog Festival in January is unmissable.
🦈 Whale sharks at Oslob✈ 1hr from Manila
Island
Coron, Palawan
Palawan · Mimaropa
Sister island to El Nido and arguably more dramatic. Kayangan Lake — the Philippines' cleanest lake — is a jaw-dropping sight. Add WWII Japanese shipwreck diving, Twin Lagoon and the russet-coloured Coron Town viewpoint. The snorkelling in Barracuda Lake is otherworldly.
🤿 Asia's best wreck diving✈ 1hr from Manila
Nature Wonder
Puerto Princesa Underground River
Palawan · Southern Luzon
A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New Seven Wonders of Nature. An 8.2km navigable underground river flowing through cathedral-like caves. The boat tour through the darkness, with bats swooping overhead and stalactites glittering above, is genuinely awe-inspiring.
🌍 UNESCO + New7Wonders✈ 1hr flight from Manila
Historic
Vigan
Ilocos Sur · Northern Luzon
Asia's best-preserved Spanish colonial city. Cobblestone Calle Crisologo is lined with 16th-century ancestral homes, horse-drawn kalesas and glowing street lamps. A living museum of the Philippines' Spanish heritage. Christmas here — with the streets lit and the churches packed — is extraordinary.
🏛 UNESCO World Heritage🚌 8hrs from Manila / 1hr flight
Highlands
Sagada & The Cordilleras
Mountain Province · Northern Luzon
A cool mountain escape in the Cordillera highlands — famous for the eerie Hanging Coffins of Sagada, limestone cave spelunking and misty rice terraces. The air is crisp, the rice wine flows and the sunrises over the valley are among the most beautiful in Southeast Asia.
🏔 1,500m elevation🚌 8hrs from Manila
Heritage
Banaue Rice Terraces
Ifugao · Northern Luzon
2,000-year-old rice terraces carved by the Ifugao people into the Cordillera mountains — the "Eighth Wonder of the World." Stay with an Ifugao family, trek the living terraces and watch sunrise turn the paddies gold. A profoundly humbling and beautiful sight.
🌍 UNESCO World Heritage🚌 9hrs from Manila
Beach
Nacpan Beach, Palawan
El Nido · Palawan
A 4km twin beach 45 minutes from El Nido town — quieter, more raw, less visited than the lagoon tours. Twin-peaked palms lean over undisturbed sand. Often voted one of the Philippines' most beautiful beaches. Go at sunrise or late afternoon for the light at its finest.
🚤 45min from El Nido town🏖 Best at sunrise
★ Our December itinerary covers Manila (Dec 24–25), El Nido (Dec 26–28) and Boracay (Dec 29–Jan 2). The destinations above not on our route — Siargao, Bohol, Cebu, Coron, Vigan, Sagada and Banaue — are all worth considering as extensions or future Philippines trips. The Philippines is big enough to fill months of travel.
Eat Your Way Through the Philippines

Filipino Food —
A Christmas Feast & Beyond

Filipino cuisine is a glorious collision of Spanish, Chinese, Malay and American influences — bold, rich, unapologetically flavourful. December is the best time to eat here: Noche Buena tables groan with lechon and bibingka, street BBQ stalls fire up every evening, and halo-halo cools you down under the tropical sun. From Anthony Bourdain's "best pig ever" to Jollibee's legendary Chickenjoy — there's something for everyone, at every budget.

Main Dish
Lechon
The king of the Filipino table
Whole roasted pig, slow-turned over charcoal for hours until the skin shatters like glass and the meat melts. Anthony Bourdain called Cebu lechon "the best pig I've ever had." The centrepiece of every Noche Buena feast — you will understand the hype.
🔥 Must-try in Cebu 💰 ~₱350–500 per portion
National Dish
Chicken Adobo
The soul of Filipino cooking
Chicken (or pork) slow-braised in vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, bay leaves and black pepper until deeply savoury and falling off the bone. Every household has their version. Served with garlic fried rice, this is the dish that will follow you home.
⭐ Everywhere 💰 ~₱80–150
Street Food
Pork BBQ Skewers
Inasal-style grilled pork on a stick
Marinated in a sweet-savoury soy-calamansi glaze and grilled over charcoal, these skewers are the Philippines' ultimate street snack. Found at every corner, market and beachside stall. Get three, hand over ₱30, and watch the sunset. Simple perfection.
🍢 Beach stalls & markets 💰 ~₱10–15 per stick
Bar Classic
Sisig
Sizzling chopped pork — the ultimate pulutan
Chopped pig's cheek, ears and liver seasoned with calamansi, chilli and onions, served sizzling on a cast-iron plate. Invented in Pampanga, now beloved nationwide. The crackling, charred edges are the best part — order it with a cold San Miguel.
🍺 Best with cold beer 💰 ~₱150–250
Comfort Food
Sinigang
Tamarind sour soup — the Filipino hug
A bright, tangy broth made with tamarind and loaded with pork ribs (or prawns or fish), kangkong, eggplant and long beans. Sour, savoury, deeply comforting. Voted one of the world's best soups. One spoonful and you'll understand why Filipinos call it home.
🥣 Every Filipino restaurant 💰 ~₱150–280
Christmas Table
Pancit
Noodles that bring long life
Stir-fried rice or glass noodles with vegetables, shrimp, pork and soy sauce. A non-negotiable at every Filipino celebration — noodles symbolise long life. You'll eat this at Noche Buena on Christmas Eve alongside the lechon. Don't cut the noodles. Bad luck.
🎄 Christmas Eve essential 💰 ~₱80–180
Slow Cooked
Kare-Kare
Rich oxtail stew in peanut sauce
Slow-braised oxtail and tripe in a thick, golden peanut sauce with banana blossom, eggplant and long beans. Always served with bagoong alamang (fermented shrimp paste) whose pungency cuts brilliantly through the richness. A dish you'll be thinking about for days.
🥜 Pampanga origin 💰 ~₱250–400
Dessert Icon
Halo-Halo
"Mix-mix" — the Philippines' beloved chaos dessert
Shaved ice drenched in evaporated milk, topped with sweet beans, jackfruit, coconut jelly, leche flan, ube ice cream and whatever else is going. It's a beautiful mess. Translates as "mix mix" — which is exactly what you do. The perfect antidote to December heat.
🍧 Best at Milky Way Café, Manila 💰 ~₱80–150
Christmas Only
Bibingka
The unmistakable scent of Christmas morning
Soft, eggy rice cake baked in banana leaves with salted egg and grated coconut. Sold exclusively outside churches during the Simbang Gabi dawn masses (Dec 16–24). The smell of bibingka cooking on charcoal at 4am is one of the great sensory experiences of Filipino Christmas.
⛪ Outside churches at dawn 💰 ~₱50–80
Christmas Only
Puto Bumbong
Steamed purple rice — Christmas in a bite
Deep violet sticky rice steamed inside bamboo tubes, served with butter, grated coconut and muscovado sugar. Vivid purple, slightly chewy, faintly sweet. Only appears at Christmas time alongside bibingka. The colour alone makes it feel like a festival food from another world.
💜 Dec 16–24 only 💰 ~₱40–70
Street Snack
Lumpiang Shanghai
The party spring roll — always on the Noche Buena table
Thin, crispy deep-fried spring rolls packed with seasoned pork and vegetables. A staple at every Filipino party and Christmas table. Dipped in sweet chilli sauce, these disappear faster than they're made. Order extra — you will regret if you don't.
🎄 Noche Buena staple 💰 ~₱5–10 each
Grill House
Chicken Inasal
Bacolod-style lemongrass grilled chicken
Chicken marinated in lemongrass, ginger, calamansi and annatto, then grilled over charcoal and basted in seasoned butter. The Bacolod version is the original and still the best — juicy, smoky, slightly tart. Eat with garlic rice and a cup of the drippings for dipping.
🍗 Best in Bacolod, also Mang Inasal 💰 ~₱100–180

🐝 Jollibee — The Philippines' Beloved Fast Food King

No food guide to the Philippines is complete without Jollibee. This homegrown fast food chain beats McDonald's here and has a near-religious following. The Chickenjoy — crispy fried chicken with gravy — is legendary. Try the spaghetti with sweet Filipino-style meat sauce. It's an experience unlike anything you've had before, and you'll find one in every city, airport, and town across the islands.

  • 🍗
    Chickenjoy — Crispy fried chicken, the icon. ~₱89
  • 🍝
    Jolly Spaghetti — Sweet Filipino-style pasta. ~₱65
  • 🍔
    Yumburger — Classic Filipino-style burger. ~₱59
  • 🥧
    Peach Mango Pie — Hot, crispy, tropical. ~₱45
  • 🌮
    Palabok Fiesta — Noodles in prawn sauce. ~₱95

🍌 Eat on Banana Leaf

Order a Boodle Fight — all dishes spread on banana leaves, eating with your hands. The ultimate communal Filipino dining experience. Ask any resort or restaurant to arrange one.

🥤 Drinks to Try

San Miguel Pale Pilsen (the local beer), fresh buko (coconut water), calamansi juice, and taho — warm silken tofu with syrup and tapioca pearls sold by street vendors each morning.

🛵 Street Food Safety

Busy stalls with high turnover are your best bet. Grilled skewers, fresh lumpia and bibingka from outside churches are all safe and delicious. Avoid anything that's been sitting out long.

💜 The Ube Obsession

Ube (purple yam) flavours everything — ice cream, pastries, pancakes, cake. It's sweet, earthy and uniquely Filipino. Try ube halaya (jam) and ube pandesal (purple bread rolls) for breakfast.

✦ The final argument ✦
Not Convinced
Yet?

We asked AI to imagine what our Christmas in the Philippines would look like. This is what it dreamed up. Imagine what the real thing could be.

Your family, together in the Philippines 🌅

Imagine this — all of you, gathered at a tropical sunset. The palm trees, the warm glow, the laughter. This could be your Christmas.

Celebrating in Manila with a Jeepney 🎉

Manila by night, Filipino flags flying, a Jeepney behind you. The city that celebrates Christmas louder than anywhere on earth.

First Jollibee. The Chickenjoy moment 🐝

Every visitor to the Philippines has their Jollibee moment. The Chickenjoy is legendary. Trust the process.

Mabuhay! Welcome to the Philippines 🌺

The whole crew, together, under the sign. "Mabuhay" means welcome — and they mean it with their whole heart.

The moment you land in Manila ✈️

Fresh off the plane, together, grinning. The trip begins. Manila Airport is your gateway to 7,641 islands of magic.

⚠️ AI Image Disclaimer — All photographs on this page are AI-generated illustrations and are not accurate depictions of real people, places or events. They are creative visualisations only, intended to inspire. For the real thing — book your tickets to the Philippines and capture memories that no AI could ever dream up. 🇵🇭

So — are we going to Pilipinas? 🎄

You've seen the Christmas traditions, the islands, the food, the flights, the budget. Noche Buena on Dec 24, island hopping through El Nido, New Year's Eve on Boracay White Beach. Seven of us, from seven cities — converging on the most joyful Christmas destination on earth.
